requirement: Buyer can create a purchase order for approved suppliers.
Supplier information is accurately captured and stored in the Coupa system.
Supplier approval process is automated and integrated with the Coupa system.
Supplier performance is monitored and evaluated in the Coupa system.
Supplier communication and collaboration are facilitated through the Coupa system.
Supplier onboarding process is streamlined and automated in the Coupa system.
Supplier data is accurately and securely stored in the Coupa system.
Supplier spend is accurately tracked and reported in the Coupa system.
Supplier compliance with policies and regulations is monitored and enforced in the Coupa system.
Carsome can manage suppliers centrally, with standardization and visibility into organizational spend.
Carsome can track suppliers, requisitions, orders, invoices, and payments in real-time, enabling better decision-making and budget control.
Coupa is integrated with Carsome's existing ERP system (NetSuite) for critical master data synchronization.
Carsome will conduct change management activities to support the adoption of new processes and the Coupa system by all relevant users.
Data migration from legacy systems to Coupa will be planned and executed as a separate workstream.
Carsome will review, cleanse, and govern key master data elements (e.g., supplier master, user data, other master data) to ensure data quality and consistency.
Carsome will review and update existing procurement and Supplier Management policies to align with the capabilities of the Coupa system and the objectives of the supplier transformation.
Carsome's technology infrastructure will be prepared to support the cloud-based Coupa solution.
This document will be a key input for the overall project governance framework, guiding scope management, progress tracking, and decision-making throughout the implementation lifecycle.
Buyer can initiate the new supplier registration process by providing basic information.
Supplier information is accurately and completely captured during the onboarding process.
Approved suppliers are created in Coupa Supplier record after completing the onboarding process.
Supplier data is accurately and up-to-date, and visibility to certifications and contact information is ensured.
Multiple channels of managing information are eliminated, and supplier participation is facilitated.
Unvetted suppliers or services go through the proper approval channels.
Buyer can create a new supplier request with required details.
System checks for potential duplicates and highlights missing or incomplete fields before final submission.
Approval process is initiated automatically in accordance with the designated workflow after the new supplier request form is submitted.
Approvers can provide rejection comments, which are received by the requester to review and resubmit the request with necessary corrections.
Coupa auto-triggers the external onboarding form after the new supplier request form is approved.
Supplier can complete the external onboarding form by registering on the Coupa Supplier Portal or submitting required information using a secure one-time password.
Supplier is required to fill in additional details for successful onboarding as a supplier for Carsome.
Buyer can create a supplier profile with company details, bank details, and risk assessment information.
Buyer can classify a supplier as local, foreign, or individual and capture relevant details accordingly.
Buyer can capture vendor type (sole proprietorship/individual or company) and vendor classification (local, foreign, or individual).
Buyer can capture and store vendor documents (e.g. Form 9/13, Certificate of Incorporation, etc.) based on vendor classification.
Buyer can capture and store vendor agreements (e.g. Non-Disclosure Agreement) and supporting documents.
Buyer can capture and store vendor bank details (e.g. payment types, bank form, etc.).
Buyer can capture and store vendor tax information (e.g. VAT registration number, tax certificate, etc.).
Buyer can capture and store vendor risk assessment information (e.g. primary commodity, risk assessment questionnaire, etc.).
Buyer can capture and store vendor acknowledgment (e.g. anti-bribery and corruption policy, purchasing terms and conditions, etc.).
Supplier can be onboarded with required details such as company name, address, contact information, and bank details.
Supplier can be classified as local, foreign, or individual based on their type.
Supplier must provide required documents such as business license, certificate of incorporation, and tax residence certificate.
Supplier must acknowledge and agree to the company's anti-bribery and corruption policy.
Risk assessment questionnaire and completed sheet must be provided for suppliers.
Buyer can create a supplier profile with required details such as vendor type, classification, company name, contact information, and bank details.
Supplier can be classified as local, foreign, or individual and the system should capture relevant documents and information accordingly.
Buyer can conduct a risk assessment for the supplier and capture relevant information such as primary commodity and other commodity.
Supplier must acknowledge and agree to the company's anti-bribery and corruption policy, general terms and conditions of purchase, and other relevant policies.
System should store and manage relevant documents such as tax certificates, statutory documents, and non-disclosure agreements for suppliers.
Supplier can submit an onboarding form with required details.
Vendor Management Team validates supplier details for accuracy and completeness.
Vendor Management Team reviews and approves or rejects the onboarding form based on NDA status.
Risk Manager reviews and approves or rejects the onboarding form for High-Risk commodities.
Finance Vendor Team reviews and approves or rejects the onboarding form for supplier financial data.
Supplier can receive purchase orders via email or manually.
Invoice matching can be done at 2-way or 3-way level.
Buyer can initiate the internal registration process for a new supplier.
Internal registration process for a new supplier requires approvals from the Requester and Procurement team.
Approved internal supplier registration request is sent to the requester to register the supplier and complete due diligence steps.
Supplier details are seamlessly integrated from Coupa Supplier Information Management (SIM) into NetSuite and updated in Coupa.
Supplier's status in Coupa is updated to 'Active' after successful integration with NetSuite.
Tax Team reviews and validates tax information provided by the supplier and updates the form accordingly.
Head of Procurement verifies submitted details and approves or rejects the form based on the supplier's data.
Buyer can create a new supplier request with required details, and the system performs a duplicate check to prevent invalid additions.
The system initiates the approval process for the new supplier request based on the designated workflow.
Line Manager and Vendor Management Team can approve or reject the supplier request, and the system prompts approvers to provide rejection comments.
After approval, the Requester initiates the Internal Supplier Registration Form with detailed supplier information and submits it into the workflow.
The Requester completes the Internal Supplier Registration Form with required details, including company information, bank details, and tax registration information.
